# mc admin tools

Small client-side scripts the MC operator runs on their own box to administer
remote MC installs. Not installed on the server.

## `mc-remote`

Wrapper around the admin API at `/api/admin/api/{health,exec,file,logs,deploy}`.

Setup:

```bash
# On any MC server, fetch the auto-generated admin token:
grep MC_ADMIN_API_TOKEN /root/mission-control/.env.production

# On your operator box (Nathan's WSL, MC Ava, etc):
mkdir -p ~/.mc-admin-tokens
echo '<token>' > ~/.mc-admin-tokens/<host>.txt
chmod 600 ~/.mc-admin-tokens/<host>.txt
cp tools/mc-remote ~/bin/    # or symlink
```

Add the host to the case statement in `mc-remote` (or set `MC_REMOTE_HOSTS=host=url,...`).

Usage:

```bash
mc-remote obt health                   # liveness probe
mc-remote obt deploy                   # git pull + npm build + systemctl restart
mc-remote obt exec 'systemctl status mission-control --no-pager'
mc-remote obt file /root/mission-control/data/users.json
mc-remote obt logs mission-control
```

The admin API itself is shipped with the MC install. Token is auto-generated by
`install/mc-install.sh` (`openssl rand -hex 32`) on first install. To rotate:
edit `MC_ADMIN_API_TOKEN` in `.env.production`, restart MC, copy new token to
operator box.
